docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: Ensure your environment meets these requirements before installing vaults-syncer.

docs.title: Minimum Specifications
docs.text: Requirement Minimum Recommended CPU 1 core 2+ cores Memory (RAM) 256 MB 512 MB - 1 GB Storage 50 MB 500 MB Network 1 Mbps 10 Mbps+

docs.title: Supported Operating Systems
docs.text:
• Linux: Ubuntu 20.04+, RHEL 8+, Debian 11+, Alpine 3.17+
  • macOS: 10.14+ (Intel), 11+ (Apple Silicon)
  • Windows: Windows Server 2019+, Windows 10+
  • Cloud: AWS, Azure, GCP, any Kubernetes cluster

docs.title: Network Requirements
docs.text:
• Outbound HTTPS: Required to connect to vaults
  • Firewall: Allow outbound HTTPS (port 443)
  • DNS: Must resolve vault hostnames
  • Connectivity: Network access to all configured vault endpoints

docs.title: Runtime
docs.text:
• Go 1.26+ (if building from source; CGO_ENABLED=1 required for the sqlite3 driver)
  • Docker 20.10+ (for containerized deployment)
  • Docker Compose 2.0+ (for Compose deployments)
  • Kubernetes 1.24+ (for Kubernetes deployment)

docs.title: Azure Key Vault
docs.text:
• Azure CLI 2.30+ (optional, for local testing)
  • Azure SDK (included in binary)
  • Network: Connectivity to *.vault.azure.net
  • Authentication: One of:
  • Microsoft Entra ID (Managed Identity)
  • Service Principal
  • Client Certificate
  • User Authentication

docs.title: Bitwarden
docs.text:
• OAuth2 Support: Bitwarden Server 2022.12+
  • Network: HTTPS connectivity to Bitwarden endpoint
  • Authentication: OAuth2 credentials (client ID + secret)

docs.title: Other Vaults
docs.text: Check specific vault integration documentation for: - Required API versions - Authentication mechanisms - Network endpoints

docs.title: Source Vault (Azure Key Vault)
docs.text:
Permissions Needed:
├── Get Secret
├── List Secrets
├── Get Key Properties (for key syncing)
└── Optional: Delete (for cleanup operations)

Configure with Azure RBAC:

# Create custom role
az role definition create --role-definition '{
  "Name": "AKV Sync Reader",
  "Description": "Role for vaults-syncer",
  "Type": "CustomRole",
  "Actions": [
    "Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/secrets/read",
    "Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/keys/read"
  ],
  "NotActions": [],
  "AssignableScopes": ["/subscriptions/{subscription-id}"]
}'

# Assign to Managed Identity or Service Principal
az role assignment create \
  --role "AKV Sync Reader" \
  --assignee-object-id <identity-object-id> \
  --scope /subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/{vault}

docs.title: Target Vault (Bitwarden)
docs.text:
Permissions Needed:
├── Admin Access (for user/collection management)
├── Cipher Read & Write
├── Folder Create & Update
└── Collection Manage

Configure OAuth2 application:

  1. Access Bitwarden admin panel
  2. Create new OAuth2 integration
  3. Grant minimum necessary scopes:
  4. cipher:read
  5. cipher:write
  6. folder:read
  7. folder:write
  8. Generate credentials

docs.title: Local Filesystem
docs.text:
• State Files: ~1-10 MB
  • Cache: ~10-50 MB
  • Logs: 10-100 MB (depends on log retention)
  • Total: Minimum 50 MB free space

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
The daemon supports three database backends (configured via DB_TYPE):

  • SQLite (embedded, default — no extra setup needed; file path via DB_PATH)
  • PostgreSQL 12+ (DB_TYPE=postgres, connection string via DB_DSN)
  • Microsoft SQL Server 2019+ (DB_TYPE=mssql, connection string via DB_DSN)

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text:
• Prometheus Endpoint: HTTP port 9090
  • Metrics Retention: Configurable (default: 30 days)

docs.title: Logging
docs.text:
• Log Destination: stdout, file, or centralized logging
  • Storage: 10-100 MB depending on retention
  • Formats: JSON, text, structured

docs.title: Alerting
docs.text:
• Prometheus AlertManager (optional)
  • Email notifications (optional)
  • Webhook integrations (optional)

docs.title: Encryption
docs.text:
• Credentials: Must be encrypted at rest
  • TLS: HTTPS for all communication (TLS 1.2+)
  • Key Rotation: Supported and recommended

docs.title: Access Control
docs.text:
• Authentication: Use managed identities or service principals
  • RBAC: Implement least-privilege principles
  • Audit Logging: Enable and retain for compliance

docs.title: Secrets Management
docs.text:
• Environment Variables: For sensitive config
  • Secret Stores: Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault, etc.
  • No Plaintext: Never store credentials in config files

docs.title: Vault APIs
docs.text:
All vaults must support:

REST API (HTTPS)
├── Authentication endpoint
├── List secrets/items endpoint
├── Get secret/item endpoint
├── Create/Update secret endpoint (for write operations)
└── Delete secret endpoint (optional)

docs.title: HTTP Compatibility
docs.text:
• HTTP/1.1 minimum
  • HTTP/2 supported
  • Keep-alive connections required

docs.title: Port Requirements
docs.text:
Default ports (configurable):

Service Port Protocol Purpose API Server + Web UI 8080 HTTP REST API endpoints and Web UI Metrics 9090 HTTP Prometheus metrics (separate server)

docs.title: Firewall Rules
docs.text:
# Allow inbound
- 8080/TCP (API access)
- 9090/TCP (Metrics scraping)

# Allow outbound
- 443/TCP (HTTPS to vaults)
- 53/UDP (DNS)

docs.title: Optional Services
docs.text:
• OpenTelemetry: For distributed tracing
  • DataDog/New Relic: For APM
  • CloudWatch/Azure Monitor: For cloud logging

These are optional and can be enabled via configuration.

docs.title: Data Protection
docs.text:
• GDPR: Data retention policies supported
  • HIPAA: Authentication and audit logging
  • SOC 2: Supported with proper configuration

docs.title: Audit Requirements
docs.text:
• Log Retention: Configurable
  • Audit Trail: All operations logged
  • Immutable Logs: Can be sent to external storage

docs.title: For 100 Secrets
docs.text:
CPU: <1 core (minimal usage)
Memory: ~200 MB
Storage: ~5 MB
Network: <1 Mbps
Sync Time: <5 seconds

docs.title: For 10,000 Secrets
docs.text:
CPU: 1-2 cores
Memory: ~500 MB
Storage: ~50 MB
Network: 5-10 Mbps
Sync Time: 30-60 seconds

docs.title: For 100,000+ Secrets
docs.text:
CPU: 2-4 cores
Memory: 1-2 GB
Storage: 100-500 MB
Network: 10+ Mbps
Sync Time: 5-10 minutes
Recommend: Pagination/batching

docs.title: Pre-Installation Checklist
docs.text:
• System meets minimum requirements
  • Network connectivity verified to all vaults
  • Credentials obtained and stored securely
  • Necessary permissions granted in source/target vaults
  • Ports 8080, 9090 available (or configured differently)
  • TLS certificates valid and up-to-date
  • Storage capacity adequate
  • Backup strategy planned
  • Monitoring and alerting configured
  • Compliance requirements understood

docs.title: ⚙️ Basic Configuration
docs.text:
The daemon stores configuration in its database. Use the Web UI at http://localhost:8080 or the admin API to add vaults and syncs. Below is the data format used by the API.

// POST /api/config/vaults
{
  "id": "source",
  "type": "azure",
  "endpoint": "https://myvault.vault.azure.net/secrets",
  "auth": {
    "method": "bearer",
    "headers": {"token": "${AZURE_ACCESS_TOKEN}"}
  },
  "field_names": {"name_field": "name", "value_field": "value"}
}

// POST /api/config/syncs
{
  "id": "my-sync",
  "source": "source",
  "targets": ["target"],
  "sync_type": "unidirectional",
  "schedule": "0 * * * *",
  "enabled": true
}

docs.title: 🔐 Authentication Quick Reference
docs.text: Vault Recommended Auth Setup Complexity Azure Key Vault Bearer Token (Azure AD) Medium Bitwarden OAuth2 Medium HashiCorp Vault X-Vault-Token header Medium AWS Secrets Manager Bearer Token / Custom Medium

docs.title: 📅 Common Schedules
docs.text:
# Every hour
schedule: "0 * * * *"

# Every 4 hours
schedule: "0 */4 * * *"

# Daily at 2 AM
schedule: "0 2 * * *"

# Monday at 3 AM
schedule: "0 3 * * 1"

# Every weekday at 9 AM
schedule: "0 9 * * 1-5"

# Every 30 minutes
schedule: "*/30 * * * *"

docs.title: 🔍 Useful Commands
docs.text:
# Check version
./sync-daemon --version

# Validate database connection only
./sync-daemon -dry-run

# Get an auth token (all API calls below require this)
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/auth/login \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"your-password"}' | jq -r .token)

# Check health (authenticated)
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8080/api/health

# List vaults
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8080/api/vaults

# List syncs
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8080/api/syncs

# Get sync status
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8080/api/syncs/{sync-id}/status

# View run history
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8080/api/syncs/{sync-id}/runs

# Run sync now
curl -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8080/api/syncs/{sync-id}/execute

# View metrics (no auth, port 9090)
curl http://localhost:9090/metrics | grep sync

# Check logs
docker logs vaults-syncer
docker compose logs -f sync-daemon

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Start Steps
docs.text:
1. Create config file → config.yaml
  2. Run with Docker → docker run ...
  3. Verify → curl http://localhost:8080/api/setup (public, no auth)
  4. Check status → curl http://localhost:8080/syncs

docs.title: ✅ Pre-Flight Checklist
docs.text:
Before running in production:

  • Read Requirements
  • Verify network access to all vaults
  • Test authentication credentials
  • Create test sync first
  • Verify sync works as expected
  • Set up monitoring/alerts
  • Enable audit logging
  • Document your sync topology
  • Create backup procedure
  • Plan credential rotation

docs.title: Application won't start
docs.text:
# Check logs
docker logs vaults-syncer

# Validate database connection
./sync-daemon -dry-run

docs.title: Sync not running
docs.text:
1. Get a token: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"username":"admin","password":"pass"}' | jq -r .token)
  2. Check health: curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:8080/api/health
  3. Verify vaults are accessible
  4. Check schedule syntax (must be valid cron)
  5. Review logs: docker logs vaults-syncer

docs.title: Authentication failed
docs.text:
1. Verify credentials/tokens
  2. Check expiration
  3. Verify permissions in target vault
  4. Test connectivity to vault endpoint

docs.title: Performance issues
docs.text:
1. Check batch size in config
  2. Reduce frequency of syncs
  3. Monitor vault API response times
  4. Check network bandwidth

docs.title: 🔒 Security Checklist
docs.text:
• Use managed identities (not hardcoded credentials)
  • Store secrets in environment variables
  • Restrict config file permissions (chmod 600)
  • Use least-privilege vault permissions
  • Enable TLS for all connections
  • Enable audit logging
  • Rotate credentials regularly
  • Restrict network access to vaults

docs.title: Create first sync
docs.text:
1. Define source and target vaults in config
  2. Add sync with schedule
  3. Test with curl -X POST .../syncs/{id}/run
  4. Verify in target vault

docs.title: Enable bidirectional sync
docs.text:
syncs:
  - id: bi-sync
    source: vault1
    targets: [vault2]
    sync_type: bidirectional

docs.title: Filter secrets
docs.text:
syncs:
  - id: filtered
    source: source
    targets: [target]
    sync_type: unidirectional
    filter:
      patterns:
        - "prod-*"
      exclude:
        - "*"

docs.title: Transform names
docs.text:
syncs:
  - id: transform
    source: source
    targets: [target]
    sync_type: unidirectional
    transforms:
      - field: value
        type: base64_encode

docs.title: 📊 API Quick Ref
docs.text:
All endpoints under /api/ (except login and setup) require Authorization: Bearer <token>.

POST /api/auth/login          - Login and get token (no auth)
GET  /api/setup               - Check setup status (no auth)
POST /api/setup               - Complete first-time setup (no auth)
GET  /api/health              - Health check
GET  /api/vaults              - List vaults
GET  /api/syncs               - List all syncs
GET  /api/syncs/{id}/status   - Get sync status
GET  /api/syncs/{id}/runs     - Sync run history
POST /api/syncs/{id}/execute  - Run sync now
GET  /api/metrics             - Prometheus metrics (also on :9090/metrics without auth)
GET  /api/config/vaults       - List vault configs (admin)
POST /api/config/vaults       - Create vault (admin)
GET  /api/config/syncs        - List sync configs (admin)
POST /api/config/syncs        - Create sync (admin)
